flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the Decimal Point?
flashcards.back: It acts as a divider separating the whole number places on the left from the fractional part places on the right.
The first place to the right of the decimal point, representing one-tenth (1/10 or 0.1) of a whole.
The second place to the right of the decimal point, representing one-hundredth (1/100 or 0.01) of a whole.
The third place to the right of the decimal point, representing one-thousandth (1/1000 or 0.001) of a whole.

flashcards.front: What is the expanded form of a decimal?
flashcards.back: Writing the decimal as an addition sentence showing the value of each individual digit (e.g., $1.25 = 1 + 0.2 + 0.05$).
